kpi_cards.label: LME CASH ($/t)
kpi_cards.delta: Latest OFFICIAL (Mon 06-Jul, Westmetall): $3,105.00 vs 03-Jul $3,080.00 = +$25.00 (+0.8%) — a SECOND straight up-day, holding above the $3,077 (.50 retrace). Tuesday 07-Jul officials post ~13:20 London. Still ~19% below the 02-Jun four-year high ($3,855).

kpi_cards.label: LME 3-MONTH ($/t)
kpi_cards.delta: 06-Jul official $3,113.00 vs 03-Jul $3,087.00 (+$26.00, +0.8%); curve a shallow -$8.00 contango. Two up-days confirm the base at the .50 retracement ($3,077); the count now needs follow-through above $3,260 (.382) — the next upgrade trigger — with $3,050 the immediate support.

kpi_cards.label: LME STOCK (t)
kpi_cards.delta: 06-Jul official 295,550 t (Westmetall) — a further ~3,225 t draw, extending the slide below the ~300kt days-of-cover threshold; ~92% Russian-origin, ~1.2 days of global output. Continuous draws through and past the correction keep the structural-deficit signal intact.

benchmark.name: LME Cash settlement
benchmark.note: Latest OFFICIAL Mon 06-Jul (Westmetall): $3,105.00 vs 03-Jul $3,080.00 = +$25.00 (+0.8%) — a second consecutive gain, holding above the $3,077 (.50 retrace). Tuesday 07-Jul officials post ~13:20 London. ~19% below the 02-Jun four-year high ($3,855). avg = June-2026 monthly mean ($3,458.6).

benchmark.name: LME 3-Month
benchmark.note: 06-Jul official $3,113.00 vs 03-Jul $3,087.00 (+$26.00, +0.8%). Two up-days confirm the base at the .50 retracement; the next upgrade trigger is a break above $3,260 (.382). China spot firmed to ¥22,840 (SMM A00, 06-Jul, +0.35%). avg = June-2026 mean ($3,438.9).

benchmark.name: Cash–3M spread
benchmark.note: 06-Jul official: cash $3,105.00 vs 3M $3,113.00 = -$8.00 = shallow CONTANGO, a touch wider than 03-Jul's -$7.00. Acute scarcity fears remain unwound while sub-296kt stock and steady draws keep the front end supported — no stress signal either way.

benchmark.name: SHFE front-month (excl VAT)
benchmark.note: SHFE front-month last posted settle ¥22,660 (03-Jul, +1.60%) ≈ $2,962/t excl-VAT at USD/CNY 6.7702 on the public Worthwill tracker; Monday 06-Jul SHFE settle not yet posted there. China spot firmed on 06-Jul: SMM A00 ¥22,840 (+0.35%), Changjiang ¥22,830 (+0.31%). With LME 3M up to $3,113, SHFE now sits ~$151 UNDER the LME 3M — the import window is more firmly shut. avg = 30-day SHFE mean ≈ ¥23,837 ≈ $3,117 (Worthwill/SHFE).

premiums.name: MJP Q3 (Main Japanese Port)
premiums.src: Q3-2026 SETTLED at $395/t (Rio Tinto & South32 with at least one Japanese buyer, 03-Jul) — +12% q/q vs Q2's $350–353 and far above the ~$275–320 consensus, though below initial $460–480 offers (Bloomberg via Mining.com). avg = Q1-26 settlement ($195).

premiums.name: CIF Japan spot
premiums.src: Last public reference ~$340 (Fastmarkets MB-AL-0343; min-tonnage spec raised to 500t eff. 25-Jun); the $395 Q3 settlement skews spot risk UPWARD, expect gradual convergence toward the settled level as post-settlement prints resume — verify Fastmarkets.

premiums.name: European Duty Unpaid (EDU)
premiums.src: EU duty-unpaid in-whs Rotterdam; easing on weak demand but underpinned by the 6% duty + CBAM. PRA prints resume this week — verify Fastmarkets MB.

premiums.name: Billets Rotterdam Duty-Paid
premiums.src: Value-added conversion premium, in-whs dp Rotterdam — conversion premia stay stickier than ingot premia — verify Fastmarkets as prints resume this week.

premiums.name: MW US Transaction (Platts)
premiums.src: Platts MW US Transaction premium; structurally elevated under the 50% Section 232 rate — the tariff-$ component alone ≈ $1,557 at the 06-Jul LME 3M ($3,113), up with the LME bounce. Verify PRA prints (US desks fully back post-holiday).

premiums.name: US Midwest Duty-Paid (P1020A)
premiums.src: Fastmarkets MB-AL-0020 ddp Midwest (~$0.20–0.21/lb market premium + 50% tariff); the tariff-$ leg rises mechanically with the LME (now $3,113) — verify PRA.

macro.note: Soft ~100 area after the June payrolls miss (+57k vs ~110k; Apr/May revised -74k) knocked September hike odds to ~50% — the dollar headwind for metals stays eased. US desks fully back post-holiday; FOMC June minutes due Wednesday (BLS / Trading Economics reference — re-validate at the US open).

macro.note: Euro firm ~1.15 as the dollar sagged post-payrolls (reference — re-validate at the US open); supportive backdrop for dollar-priced metals.

macro.name: USD/CNY
macro.note: Yuan steady ~6.77 (06-Jul reference via the public Worthwill conversion) — China export competitiveness intact; the aluminium export ramp continues (Fastmarkets sees +12–18% for 2026).

macro.name: USD/JPY
macro.note: Yen ~¥160; intervention risk persists — marginal premium support for Japan-bound cargoes.

macro.name: US 10Y yield (%)
macro.note: ~4.55 reference; the soft June payrolls print (+57k, u-rate 4.2%) trimmed Fed-hike odds — FOMC June minutes Wednesday, US June CPI mid-month are the next swings.

macro.name: Brent crude ($/bbl)
macro.note: ~$71–72; UAE exports restored >3.9 mbpd and Hormuz flows >10 mbpd keep the war premium unwound (Investing.com/Trading Economics reference).

macro.name: Natural gas ($/MMBtu)
macro.note: Direct refining input; Gulf gas-disruption risk minimal with transit normalised.

macro.name: Copper ($/lb)
macro.note: LME 3M $13,370/t (06-Jul official, +0.2%) ≈ $6.06/lb — steady on firm China data; +6.9% YTD, the resilient leader of the base complex.

macro.name: Zinc ($/t)
macro.note: LME 3M $3,582/t (06-Jul official, +1.4%) — extended Friday's rebound; the YTD outperformer (+14.4%).

macro.name: Nickel ($/t)
macro.note: LME 3M $16,250 (06-Jul official, -0.5%) — slipped back toward 2026 lows; the surplus story is intact (-3.9% YTD).

macro.name: Gold ($/oz)
macro.note: ~$4,148/oz (07-Jul, -0.4% reference) — off recent highs but the safe-haven bid persists with Fed-path uncertainty; re-validate at the US open.

macro_events: BASE CONFIRMED, TWO UP-DAYS: Monday's 06-Jul official posted $3,105.00 cash / $3,113.00 3M (+0.8/+0.8%) — a SECOND straight gain that holds the metal above the $3,077 (.50 retracement); stock drew a further ~3,225 t to 295,550 t. Tuesday 07-Jul officials post ~13:20 London — the count now needs $3,260 (.382) to upgrade (Westmetall).
BASE COMPLEX BROADLY FIRMER: full 06-Jul officials — Cu 3M $13,370 (+0.2%), Zn 3M $3,582 (+1.4%, YTD leader +14.4%); Ni 3M $16,250 (-0.5%) and Pb 3M $1,888 (-0.2%) lagged on their surplus stories.

JAPAN Q3 PREMIUM SETTLED UP, NOT DOWN: Rio Tinto and South32 agreed Q3 MJP at $395/t — above Q2's $350–353 and far above the ~$275–320 consensus (initial offers $460–480). Post-settlement CIF Japan spot (~$340) should converge upward (Bloomberg via Mining.com, 03-Jul; Fastmarkets MB-AL-0343).

GULF SUPPLY HEALING — AHEAD OF PLAN: EGA has 89 of 1,262 Al Taweelah cells back (~7% of pots), targets FIRST refinery alumina early in Q3, casthouse remelting frozen metal since 04-May; ~700kt of Gulf metal remains framed to return in H2 (EGA / Alcircle).
SECTOR CONSOLIDATION: Alcoa's agreement to acquire South32's aluminium portfolio (Worsley 86%, Hillside 100%) for up to $5.6bn (01-Jul) frames the Q2 season, which opens with Alcoa on 16-Jul.

FREIGHT TURNS UP: BDI rebounded to 2,797 on 06-Jul (+2.94%), snapping a six-session slide (capesize led) — a reversal of the recent easing in bulk freight for bauxite/alumina (Baltic Exchange / Trading Economics).
MACRO WEEK AHEAD: DXY soft ~100 (Sep hike odds ~50% after the +57k payrolls miss); FOMC June minutes Wednesday, China June CPI/PPI Thursday, US June CPI mid-month (BLS / Trading Economics).
lme_commentary: Tue 07-Jul run (pre-officials) — today's LME officials post ~13:20 London. The latest OFFICIAL close is Mon 06-Jul: $3,105.00 cash / $3,113.00 3M / stock 295,550 t (Westmetall). Monday delivered a SECOND straight up-day (+0.8/+0.8%), confirming the base that formed at the $3,077 (.50 retracement) — the curve holds a shallow -$8.00 contango and stock drew a further ~3,225 t to below 296kt. The base complex firmed alongside (Cu +0.2%, Zn +1.4%), while Ni (-0.5%) and Pb (-0.2%) lagged. The read is now a confirmed short-term base rather than a single-day bounce: the count needs a reclaim of $3,260 (.382) to upgrade toward a completed correction, while first support sits at $3,050 and a sustained break there re-opens $2,950/$2,894 (.618). China spot firmed on Monday (SMM A00 ¥22,840, +0.35%), keeping the import window shut (~$151 under LME 3M). Today's officials test whether the recovery can extend.

alumina.name: Alumina FOB Australia (SMM/Fastmarkets)
alumina.src: ~$340/t FOB East Australia; Q2/H2 oversupply still pressuring; Nalco guides $320–330/t for 2026 — verify SMM/Fastmarkets

alumina.name: Alumina API / Platts region
alumina.src: Ex-China benchmark region; easing on the building surplus — verify Platts

alumina_notes: Alumina: ex-China FOB Australia (~$340/t) softer on Q2/H2 oversupply vs Platts API region (~$304/t; FOB Australia Platts futures ~$309.4); Nalco guides $320–330/t for 2026 as Indonesia/India refinery capacity ramps into subdued demand — a building ~1 Mt+ surplus.
Gulf alumina-logistics risk has largely cleared as Hormuz transit normalises (flows >10 mbpd) — ~60% of regional alumina imports transit the Strait, and flows have rebuilt on de-escalation.

Bauxite: Guinea's oversupply keeps CIF China ~$68/dmt (FOB Guinea ~$38, 45/3 grade); Guinea weighing export curbs (a >$100/dmt target) — a structural upside risk to raw-material cost independent of the Gulf.

Refining: EGA expects FIRST ALUMINA from the Al Taweelah refinery early in Q3-2026, with the smelter hot-metal ramp decoupled from the refinery ramp-up (EGA/The National, 02-Jul) — the key ex-China supply swing to watch.
Net: alumina balance skews to surplus (ample supply caps the price); de-escalation removes the logistics overlay, leaving Guinea bauxite-curb risk as the main floor under raw-material cost.
net_read: BASE CONFIRMED (TWO UP-DAYS), STILL RANGE-BOUND. Aluminium's Hormuz-driven run to a four-year high ($3,855 cash, 02-Jun) corrected ~20% into early July, then based at the $3,077 (.50 retracement) and has now turned higher for two straight sessions: the 06-Jul OFFICIAL ($3,105.00 cash / $3,113.00 3M, +0.8/+0.8%) sits comfortably above the level, and Japan's Q3 MJP premium settled UP at $395/t (vs a ~$275–320 consensus). The physical market is still short units: LME stock drew again to sub-296kt (295,550 t; ~92% Russian-origin), China spot firmed (SMM A00 ¥22,840, +0.35%, import window shut ~$151 under LME 3M), and EGA is at only ~7% of Al Taweelah pots. The bear case stays live: EGA is AHEAD of its restart plan (first refinery alumina due early Q3), ~700kt of Gulf metal is framed for H2, China exports run +12–18%, and Indonesia's ramp anchors Goldman's 1.5/2.0 Mt 2026–27 surplus projections. US premiums stay tariff-inflated (50% Section 232; tariff-$ ≈ $1,557 at LME $3,113); DXY soft ~100 keeps the macro headwind eased; freight ticked back up (BDI 2,797, +2.9%). The recovery is real but unconfirmed as a trend — the count needs follow-through above $3,260 (.382), with $3,050 the line that would re-open the downside.

feed.text: Tuesday pre-officials check: today's LME officials post ~13:20 London. Latest OFFICIAL is Mon 06-Jul — cash $3,105.00 / 3M $3,113.00 / stock 295,550 t (Westmetall). Two straight up-days now hold the metal above the $3,077 (.50) support; the base complex is broadly firm (Cu 3M $13,370, Zn 3M $3,582) with Ni/Pb lagging. China spot firm (SMM A00 ¥22,840); import window shut (~$151 under LME 3M).

Westmetall posted the 06-Jul OFFICIAL: cash $3,105.00 / 3M $3,113.00 / stock 295,550 t — +0.8/+0.8%, a SECOND consecutive gain that confirms the base at the $3,077 (.50) retracement; stock drew a further ~3,225 t below 296kt. Curve a shallow -$8.00 contango.

Freight reversed higher: the Baltic Dry Index rebounded +2.94% to 2,797, snapping a six-session slide (capesize-led) — bulk-freight costs for bauxite/alumina tick back up after easing to a mid-April low.

Westmetall posted the 03-Jul OFFICIAL: cash $3,080.00 / 3M $3,087.00 / stock 298,775 t — +0.6/+0.7%, the first up-day since 26-Jun, closing back above the $3,077 (.50) support; stock broke below 300kt for the first time this cycle.

Q3 MJP settled at $395/t (Rio Tinto/South32 with at least one Japanese buyer) — +12% q/q vs Q2's $350–353 and far above the ~$275–320 consensus range; initial offers were $460–480 (Bloomberg via Mining.com).
EGA: ~7% of Al Taweelah pots back online; exporting from inventories with stockpiles targeted back to normal by end-2026; aiming to accelerate restoration (CFO interview).
BDI -1.4% to 2,490 — sixth straight decline, lowest since 15-Apr (capesize -2.8% to 3,538) — dry-bulk freight costs easing (Cyprus Shipping News).
US June payrolls +57k vs ~110k expected, u-rate 4.2%, -74k back revisions (BLS) — DXY slid to ~100.8, Sep hike odds to ~50%; the dollar headwind on metals eased into the July-4 weekend.

Alcoa agreed to acquire South32's aluminium portfolio (Worsley 86%, Hillside 100%) for up to $5.6bn ($3.1bn cash + $1bn stock + $750m contingent to 2030) — major ex-China supply consolidation (Reuters/Bloomberg).

SMM: China ingot social inventory fell another ~40kt w/w as the lower price drew out downstream buying; primary output keeps rising on new capacity/restarts and stranded-wire exports flow to Korea/Japan.

news.headline: Q3 MJP SETTLED AT $395/t — Rio Tinto and South32 agreed the third-quarter premium with at least one Japanese buyer at $395 over LME, UP from Q2's $350–353 and far above the ~$275–320 consensus, though below initial $460–480 offers. Japan's Middle-East dependence is forcing buyers to pay up despite the LME's ~20% correction.

Aluminium confirmed its base at the .50 retracement: the 06-Jul Westmetall OFFICIAL closed $3,105.00 cash / $3,113.00 3M (+0.8/+0.8%) — a SECOND straight up-day — and LME stock drew again to 295,550 t (sub-296kt). The count now needs $3,260 (.382) to upgrade toward a completed correction.

EGA is running AHEAD of its internal restart timeline at Al Taweelah: 89 of the site's 1,262 reduction cells (~7% of pots) are back online, anode removal is complete and bath cleaning ~90% done; hot-metal ramp to pre-incident levels could take up to a year. Exports continue from inventories; the ~700kt H2 Gulf-return framing stands — the supply floor erodes gradually, not suddenly.

Alcoa agreed to buy South32's aluminium portfolio (Worsley Alumina 86%, Hillside Aluminium 100%) for up to $5.6bn — $3.1bn cash + $1bn stock (+ up to $750m contingent on prices to 2030). A major consolidation of ex-China alumina/aluminium supply announced 01-Jul.

June payrolls missed at +57k (vs ~110k; Apr/May revised -74k; u-rate 4.2%) — DXY eased to ~100.8 and September hike odds fell to ~50% from ~64%. First supportive macro impulse since the correction began; US markets were closed Friday (July-4 observed).
news.source: BLS Employment Situation (02-Jul) — bls.gov; Trading Economics

news.headline: Hydro will close two US aluminium extrusion plants — City of Industry (CA) by early 2027 and Delhi (LA) through Q3-2027, ~350 jobs — citing below-capacity running rates and heavy reinvestment needs. A reminder that record-high US all-in prices (tariff-inflated Midwest) are pressuring downstream demand even as upstream stays tight.
news.source: Hydro press release / Aluminium International Today (this week) — hydro.com/us/global/media/news/2026/hydro-to-close-two-extrusion-plants-in-the-u.s

news.headline: Chinese smelters and merchants are maximising exports of stranded aluminium wire (much of it remelted abroad into ingot), with Korean and Japanese buyers active per customs data; ingot social inventory still fell ~40kt w/w as lower prices drew out buying.
BDI rebounded +2.94% to 2,797 on 06-Jul, snapping a six-session slide (capesize-led) — bulk-freight costs for bauxite/alumina tick back up after easing to a mid-April low of 2,490.

Three Chinese-backed smelters near completion in Indonesia (Xinfa–Tsingshan Juwan at Weda Bay, Taijing at Morowali, Adaro–Lygend Kaltara) — Indonesian primary output is projected to roughly double from 815kt (2025) to ~1.6Mt in 2026; Goldman Sachs projects a 1.5Mt global surplus in 2026 widening to 2Mt in 2027.

China stayed firm into Tuesday: SMM A00 spot ¥22,840 (06-Jul, +0.35%), Changjiang ¥22,830 (+0.31%); the last posted SHFE settle is ¥22,660 (03-Jul, ≈$2,962/t ex-VAT). With the LME 3M up to $3,113, SHFE now sits ~$151 under — the import window is more firmly shut. Ingot social inventory ~1.165 Mt after last week's ~170kt draw, the largest in nearly four years, as lower prices pull out downstream buying.
news.source: SHFE/SMM prints via Worthwill public tracker (06-Jul) — worthwillaluminium.com/aluminum-price/; SMM (metal.com)

earnings.headline: Revenue $3.2bn (−7% q/q); net income $425m ($1.60/sh); adj. EBITDA $595m; cash $1.4bn.
earnings.readthrough: Higher Al prices + operational resilience amid Gulf disruption; San Ciprián restart completed Apr 2026. 01-Jul: agreed to acquire South32's aluminium portfolio (Worsley 86%, Hillside 100%) for up to $5.6bn ($3.1bn cash + $1bn stock + $750m contingent to 2030). Q2 results due 16-Jul (after NYSE close; call 5pm EDT).

earnings.headline: Adj. EBITDA NOK 8,668m (vs 9,516m yr-ago); adj. EPS NOK 2.07 (beat, up from 1.63).
earnings.readthrough: Primary Al EBITDA +2.7% YoY as Norwegian smelters reopened; soft alumina prices & stronger NOK; Extrusions outlook cut. Q2 due 22-Jul (07:00 CEST).

earnings.headline: Adj. EPS $1.63 (beat $1.56); revenue $649m; net income $327m (incl. $288m Hawesville sale gain).
earnings.readthrough: Higher LME + strong US Midwest premiums; Q2 adj. EBITDA guided $315–335m.

earnings.headline: Net profit $200.3m (+316% YoY); EPS 53 fils; sales −17% on Gulf supply-chain disruption.
earnings.readthrough: LME averaged $3,195/t (+22% YoY) drove profit surge; volumes hit by regional conflict; ~19% capacity in controlled shutdown — restart path improves post-truce.

earnings.headline: Net profit SAR 1.63bn (~$436m, +5.5% YoY); revenue SAR 8.79bn (+3.2%); EBITDA $964m.
earnings.readthrough: Strong pricing across commodities; partly offset by lower phosphate volumes; 2026 capex set >$4bn.

earnings.headline: Aluminium 835kt (+1% YoY); alumina +6%; bauxite −11% (Cyclone Narelle at Weipa/Gove); AP60 first hot metal.
earnings.readthrough: Flags Gulf curtailments = 23% of ex-China supply; expects enhanced 2026 deficit; Kitimat/AP60 ramp-up.

earnings.headline: Hillside Al ~538kt (9M, FY26 guide 720kt); Brazil Alumina record 1,060kt (9M); Worsley steady.
earnings.readthrough: Alumina sales −15% q/q on availability/timing; annual guidance intact despite cyclone gas disruption.

earnings.headline: Hindalco PAT ₹5,283cr (+66% YoY); India Al upstream EBITDA margin ~46%; Novelis FY26 adj. EBITDA $1.6bn (−9%).
earnings.readthrough: Record India operations; Novelis pressured by Oswego outage (~$104m) and tariffs (~$143m).

earnings.headline: Net profit guided RMB 5.30–5.59bn (+50–58% YoY).
earnings.readthrough: Strong on higher Al prices; China output near self-imposed 45 Mt capacity cap.

earnings.headline: Underlying profit $6.2bn (+>20%); underlying EBITDA $15.5bn (+25%, 58% margin); interim div 73c.
earnings.readthrough: Copper-led strength; broad base-metals read-across positive for the complex.

earnings.headline: FY25 results; copper & aluminium average prices +9% YoY; zinc metal +4%.
earnings.readthrough: Resilient structural demand for Cu/Al cited; broadly constructive sector tone.

earnings.headline: FY25 net loss $455m (vs $803m profit in 2024) on higher costs & lower output.
earnings.readthrough: Analysts expect stronger H1 2026 on higher prices; Asia pivot amid fractured trade flows.

earnings_note: Earnings are quarterly/episodic; entries show each peer's most recent reported results as of 07-Jul (no new releases since). Q2 season dates: Alcoa 16-Jul (after NYSE close; call 5pm EDT), Rio Tinto Q2 production report mid-Jul, Norsk Hydro 22-Jul (07:00 CEST), Maaden (1211) late-Jul/early-Aug. Corporate action: Alcoa–South32 aluminium-portfolio deal (up to $5.6bn) announced 01-Jul. Sources: company releases & SEC/exchange filings, Reuters, Bloomberg, BusinessWire, Alcircle.

commercial.text: Two up-days off the .50 retrace ($3,077; 3M now $3,113, +0.8%) confirm a short-term base — but it is not yet a confirmed uptrend. Keep LME hedges in place on priced volume; pace unpriced tonnes into strength rather than chasing — a reclaim of $3,260 (.382) is the trigger that upgrades the count, while $3,050 is the line that re-opens downside. Defend value-added (billet/slab/PFA) premium capture: conversion premia stay stickier than ingot premia.

The Q3 MJP settlement at $395/t (vs Q2 $350–353 and a ~$275–320 consensus) resets the Asian benchmark UP — anchor Q3 renewals and spot offers to it; CIF Japan spot (~$340) should converge upward as post-settlement prints resume. US MW ~$2,405/t stays structurally high on the 50% Section 232 rate (tariff-$ ≈ $1,557 at LME $3,113, rising with the LME); EU underpinned by the 6% duty + CBAM.

Ex-China FOB Australia ~$340/t (API ~$304; Platts futures ~$309) with the Q2/H2 surplus capping upside; Nalco guides $320–330 for 2026. If net seller, transact into any firmness promptly; if buyer for smelting, the surplus caps delivered cost — though freight just turned up (BDI +2.9% to 2,797), so lock bulk windows opportunistically rather than assuming further easing.

Hormuz transit runs >10 mbpd and Gulf exports resume — keep routing normalised. Note the freight reversal: BDI rebounded +2.9% to 2,797 (six-session slide broken), so dry-bulk is no longer one-way cheaper; re-check freight assumptions on bauxite/alumina lanes. Keep war-risk cover and demurrage clauses live while Iran contests maritime administrative control; hold the Jeddah/Red Sea land-bridge on standby.

Use the Q3 MJP settlement ($395) and the two-day LME base to reset the conversation: the physical market remains tight (stock sub-296kt) even after the exchange correction — push back on discount pressure anchored to the June slide. Reaffirm reliability and OTIF, keep allocation transparent, and defend premium tolerance through the Q3 window.

Watch four confirmations this week: (1) whether the LME extends above $3,260 (.382) to upgrade the count; (2) CIF Japan spot convergence toward the $395 settlement; (3) the macro tone — FOMC June minutes Wednesday, China June CPI/PPI Thursday, US June CPI mid-month; (4) Alcoa Q2 (16-Jul) for the first producer read on Q2 price realisation.

bottom_line: On 07-Jul the base has firmed into a two-day recovery: the 06-Jul official ($3,105.00 cash / $3,113.00 3M, +0.8/+0.8%) sits above the $3,077 (.50 retracement), LME stock drew again to sub-296kt, China spot stayed firm (SMM A00 ¥22,840, import window shut), and Q3 MJP is settled UP at $395/t. Against that: EGA runs AHEAD of its Al Taweelah restart plan (first refinery alumina due early Q3), ~700kt of Gulf metal is framed to return in H2, and Indonesia's ramp underpins projected 2026–27 surpluses (1.5/2.0 Mt, Goldman). Macro helps at the margin (DXY soft ~100; Sep hike odds ~50%), though freight ticked up (BDI 2,797, +2.9%). Net: the $3,050–3,110 zone now reads as a base, not air — the recovery still needs follow-through above $3,260 (.382) to become a trend. Today's catalysts: the 07-Jul LME officials and post-settlement CIF Japan indications; then FOMC minutes Wednesday, China CPI Thursday, and Alcoa's Q2 on 16-Jul opening the earnings season.
sources: London Metal Exchange via Westmetall — westmetall.com (latest official Mon 06-Jul: Al cash $3,105.00 / 3M $3,113.00 / stock 295,550 t; Tuesday 07-Jul officials post ~13:20 London — verified at run time).

EGA Al Taweelah restart (~7% of pots; refinery alumina early Q3)

Full 06-Jul officials for the base complex are reflected (Cu 3M $13,370, Zn 3M $3,582, Ni 3M $16,250, Pb 3M $1,888).

China: the public Worthwill tracker had not yet posted the Monday 06-Jul SHFE settle at run time (last shown ¥22,660, 03-Jul); the freshest China prints used are SMM A00 spot ¥22,840 and Changjiang ¥22,830 (both 06-Jul). Verify against SMM (metal.com) before commercial use.

Macro references (DXY ~100, Brent ~$71.9, gold ~$4,148, EUR/USD ~1.15) are the latest public references; Trading Economics' commodity page was serving a stale June snapshot at run time, so FX/energy prints should be re-validated at the US open before commercial use.

PRA premiums (CIF Japan, EDU, billets, US MW) are carried at last public reference pending fresh post-settlement assessments — verify against S&P Global Platts / Fastmarkets subscriptions before commercial use.

Q3 MJP settlement ($395/t) is per Bloomberg-sourced trade reporting (Rio Tinto/South32 with at least one buyer); other supplier/buyer pairs may settle at different levels — treat as the benchmark print pending PRA confirmation.

Hormuz status: transit restored (>10 mbpd; UAE >3.9 mbpd) but 'patchy and not fully transparent' (Vanda Insights) and Iran still contests maritime administrative control — two-sided headline risk persists.

forward_path.basis: 06-Jul Westmetall official cash; +0.8% vs 03-Jul — a second straight up-day, holding above the $3,077 (.50) support.
06-Jul Westmetall official 3M; +0.8% vs 03-Jul; curve a shallow -$8.00 contango.

outlook.curve_note: The front end sits in shallow contango (-$8.00 on 06-Jul) — acute scarcity fears are unwound while steady stock draws (295,550 t, now BELOW ~296kt) keep the nearby dates supported. Consensus 2026 average sits in the $3,200–3,500 band before easing into 2027 as Gulf supply is repaired; ~700kt of Gulf metal is framed to return in H2 and China exports run +12–18%, but the Q3 MJP settlement at $395/t shows the physical market is still paying up — the structural deficit remains the floor under the $3,050–3,110 support zone.

consensus.note: Running above the World Bank base
First structural deficit in ~20 years; BofA flags near-term downside

balance.label: BofA/Morgan/BNP structural deficit; Goldman flags later surplus risk

capacity_pipeline.change: Output near the 45 Mt self-imposed cap; exports seen +12–18% (Fastmarkets), potentially ~7.2 Mt
capacity_pipeline.timing: Structural ceiling on output; rising exports

capacity_pipeline.change: New smelter capacity ramping (BofA flags as added supply)

scenarios.drivers: Gulf re-escalation / Hormuz re-closure, EGA/ALBA restart slips, deficit deepens, China stays capped
drivers_list: Geopolitics: ceasefire breaks down or Hormuz is credibly re-closed — war premium re-ignites
Supply: Gulf restart slips — EGA Al Taweelah / ALBA recovery runs beyond ~12 months, or fresh outages
Inventory: visible LME stocks draw below ~300kt — days-of-cover critical
China: output stays capped at 45 Mt; net exports fall as domestic demand absorbs metal
Targets: Citi/Goldman ~$3,600, upside ~$4,000+ only if the Strait stays shut beyond ~30 days

scenarios.drivers: Ceasefire holds; structural deficit floors price against China/Indonesia supply — elevated but drifting lower
drivers_list: Geopolitics: ceasefire/Hormuz reopening holds — no corridor risk premium
Supply: structural deficit persists (~-365 kt 2026) with Gulf capacity repairing gradually (the floor)
China: exports strong (~+12–18% YoY); output near the 45 Mt cap — the bearish counterweight
Macro: USD firm after the hawkish June FOMC; soft China demand caps rebounds
Curve: contango holds; price oscillates $3,100–3,450 around the support band

scenarios.drivers: Durable de-escalation, China + Indonesia supply surge, hawkish Fed/strong USD, demand softening
drivers_list: Geopolitics: full normalisation — Gulf restarts beat plan, no premium
Supply: China export surge plus rising Indonesian smelter output; Middle East inventories released
Macro: hawkish Fed / stronger USD; rate-hike risk weighs on dollar-priced metals
Demand: global manufacturing & construction soften; destocking sets in
Inventory: visible stocks rebuild as supply returns; contango deepens

risks.risk: China + Indonesia supply (export pace, 45 Mt cap)

catalysts.event: LME officials — can the two-day base extend toward $3,260 (.382)?
US June CPI; China June trade & aluminium export data
Alcoa Q2 earnings (after close) — sector kickoff
Norsk Hydro Q2 (07:00 CEST); Rio Tinto Q2 production mid-Jul
MJP Q3-26 premium SETTLED at $395/t (Rio Tinto/South32)

ai_analysis: What changed: Monday's 06-Jul officials delivered a SECOND straight up-day (Al 3M $3,113, +0.8%) with a further stock draw to 295,550 t — the base at the $3,077 (.50) retracement is now confirmed rather than a one-day bounce. The base complex firmed (Cu +0.2%, Zn +1.4%) while Ni (-0.5%) and Pb (-0.2%) lagged on their surplus stories.
Term structure: cash–3M at -$8.00 (06-Jul) — a shallow contango; sub-296kt stock on steady draws keeps the front end supported without acute stress.

Drivers: physical tightness (Q3 MJP $395; ~170kt China weekly destock, largest in ~4 years; import window shut ~$151 under LME) versus supply normalisation (EGA ahead of plan at ~7% of pots, first refinery alumina early Q3; ~700kt Gulf H2 return; Indonesia ramp → Goldman 1.5/2.0 Mt surpluses). Macro eased: DXY soft ~100; FOMC minutes Wednesday and China CPI Thursday are the next swings; freight turned up (BDI +2.9%).

Net base case: hold above $3,050 → extend toward $3,200, with $3,260 (.382) the trigger that upgrades the count to a completed correction; a sustained break below $3,050 re-opens $2,950/$2,894 (.618). Today's officials test whether the recovery can build a trend.

premium_explainer.desc: Quarterly-negotiated premium over LME cash for primary metal delivered to Japan — the Asian benchmark.
Spot premium for cargoes delivered cost-insurance-freight to Japan; leads the next MJP negotiation.
Premium for non-EU-origin metal in-warehouse Rotterdam before the 3% import duty — the European spot gauge.
Value-added premium for extrusion billet (conversion + logistics) on top of the ingot premium.
Delivered-Midwest premium over LME; heavily driven by US Section 232 tariffs and import economics.

premium_outlook: Asian (MJP / CIF Japan): Q3 MJP SETTLED at $395/t (+12% q/q; fourth consecutive rise: $86 → $195 → $351.5 → $395) — far above the ~$275–320 consensus, confirming physical tightness. CIF Japan spot (~$340 last public reference) should converge upward toward the settlement as post-settlement PRA prints resume.
European (EDU): ~$470/t, softer on weak demand but underpinned by the 6% duty + CBAM — the policy floor holds even as spot eases.

US Midwest: MW US Transaction ~$2,405/t; duty-paid P1020A ~$2,045/t — structurally elevated on the 50% Section 232 rate; the tariff-$ component (50% x LME ≈ $1,557 at $3,113) ticked up with Monday's LME recovery.

Common thread: the exchange price corrected ~20% but the premium complex says the physical market is still short units — Japan settled UP for Q3 while EGA runs only ~7% of Al Taweelah pots. Premium risk stays skewed higher until Gulf capacity visibly rebuilds.

alumina_explainer.desc: Refined intermediate from bauxite via the Bayer process; ~1.9–2 t alumina per 1 t aluminium. Smelter-grade is the traded form.
The ex-China benchmark (Platts Alumina Price Index) referenced in many alumina contracts.
Spot index for Australian-origin smelter-grade alumina — a key seaborne reference.
The ore refined into alumina; ~2–3 t bauxite per 1 t alumina. Guinea & Australia dominate seaborne supply.
Primary Bayer reagent that dissolves alumina from bauxite — a major refining cost (~$80–120 per t of alumina).
Used for causticisation & desilication in refining — a smaller but essential reagent cost.

raw_materials.name: Bauxite (Guinea, CIF China)
raw_materials.src: ~$68 CIF China (FOB Guinea ~$38, 45/3 grade); oversupply has roughly halved prices since Jan-25 — Guinea weighing export curbs toward a >$100/dmt target (SMM/Fastmarkets/Discovery Alert)

raw_materials.name: Caustic soda (flakes ≥98%, FOB NE Asia)
raw_materials.src: ~$500–510 FOB NE Asia; CFR Asia ~$380–560 — major refining reagent (ChemAnalyst / ChemTradeAsia)

raw_materials.name: Lime / quicklime (CaO)
raw_materials.src: No liquid global index — largely regional/contract; indicative range (causticisation reagent)

alumina_outlook: Alumina: ex-China FOB Australia (~$340/t) easing on Q2/H2 oversupply; API region ~$304 (FOB futures ~$309). Surplus building into H2; the Hormuz reopening removes the Gulf-logistics risk overlay.
Bauxite: Guinea oversupply keeps CIF China ~$68/dmt (FOB Guinea ~$38) — Guinea's export-curb push (targeting >$100/dmt) is the key upside risk to raw-material cost.
Caustic soda firm (~$500/t FOB NE Asia) — a sticky refining cost; lime stable. Net refining-cost pressure eases with alumina/bauxite while caustic keeps a floor.
Read-through: softer alumina + bauxite lower a smelter's input cost; for an alumina seller, oversupply caps realizations into Q3 with Gulf logistics risk now receding.

inputs.name: Calcined petroleum coke (CPC, anode-grade)
inputs.src: Peaked ~$896/t (2022); battery-anode demand + tight seaborne supply pressuring — CRU; limited price transparency

inputs.name: Coal tar pitch (CTP)
inputs.src: Anode binder; by-product of steel coke ovens — supply tied to steel output; thin spot market

inputs.name: Pre-baked anodes
inputs.src: Often produced in-house; China the swing exporter — mostly contract pricing

inputs.name: Electricity (smelter power)
inputs.src: Single largest cost & the competitiveness driver; no single index — PPAs / regional grid / self-gen; rising carbon-cost exposure

inputs.name: Cryolite / bath makeup
inputs.src: Largely synthetic & recycled in-cell; limited spot market

inputs.name: Cathode / graphite blocks
inputs.src: Multi-year pot-reline cost; graphite-electrode supply (China) is the risk

inputs.name: Silicon metal
inputs.src: China Q2-26 ~$1,250 FOB; EU ~$1,580, US ~$2,625 (tariff split) — SMM/ChemAnalyst

inputs.name: Magnesium (99.9%)
inputs.src: China ~85% of global supply (~$2,359 Jan-26) — concentration risk; SMM/Asian Metal

inputs.name: Copper (LME)
inputs.src: LME-traded — full transparency (3M ~$13,370/t, ~$6.06/lb, 06-Jul official, +0.2% on firm China data; COMEX near records on US tariff premium); alloy input for 2xxx & some 6xxx

inputs.name: Ti-B grain refiner (AlTi5B1)
inputs.src: Master-alloy rod; priced off titanium + conversion — mostly contract

logistics.name: Baltic Dry Index (BDI)
logistics.note: Bulk-freight proxy for bauxite/alumina; last print 2,797 (Mon 06-Jul, +2.94%) — a REBOUND that snaps the prior six-session slide (capesize-led), off the 03-Jul low of 2,490 (lowest since 15-Apr). Next Baltic Exchange print due today. Dry-bulk freight is no longer one-way cheaper (Baltic Exchange / Trading Economics).

logistics.name: Strait of Hormuz transit
logistics.val: Open — crude flows >10 mbpd; UAE exports >3.9 mbpd
logistics.note: ~80–85% of Gulf Al exports & ~60% of regional alumina imports transit Hormuz. Flows are restored (UAE >3.9 mbpd; total >10 mbpd) and the market prices ~700kt of Gulf aluminium returning in H2. Reopening remains 'patchy and not fully transparent' (Vanda Insights via Reuters), so residual two-sided headline risk persists — directional read stays bearish for premia.

logistics.name: Gulf war-risk insurance / surcharges
logistics.note: War-risk premia continue to normalise as transit holds; keep cover in place while Iran contests maritime administrative control in case of re-escalation.

logistics.name: Land-bridge (Gulf → Jeddah / Red Sea)
logistics.note: Trucking workaround — retain as a live contingency while Gulf export volumes rebuild toward normal.

logistics.name: Incoterms / demurrage
logistics.note: Re-optimise CIF vs FOB exposure and keep demurrage clauses live until routing fully normalises.

inputs_summary: Track closely (material AND tradeable): power (largest cost — watch PPAs/carbon), calcined pet coke (rising, low transparency — the biggest carbon risk), silicon & magnesium (indexed, China-concentrated), copper (LME-transparent, 3M $13,370 06-Jul official).
Low transparency / contract (monitor via proxies): coal tar pitch, pre-baked anodes, cryolite, cathodes, Ti-B — no liquid index; track through supplier contracts and China export trends.
Highest supply-concentration risk: magnesium (~85% China), CPC (Asia / battery-anode competition), AlF₃ (fluorspar/China) — candidates to diversify or contract forward.

Logistics is NORMAL but freight turned up: Hormuz transit restored (>10 mbpd) while the BDI rebounded to 2,797 (06-Jul, +2.9%), snapping a six-session slide — bulk freight for bauxite/alumina is no longer one-way cheaper; keep war-risk cover on standby.

Procurement read: power + carbon (coke/pitch) drive roughly half of conversion cost — prioritise these for hedging / forward contracting; alloying metals are smaller line items but China-concentrated.

long_term.label: Long-term — weekly (position degree)
long_term.writeup: Primary (weekly): the five-wave advance from the May-2025 low (~$2,300) topped at the $3,855 four-year high (early-Jun-2026) — a Hormuz-driven fifth-wave blow-off confirmed by bearish weekly RSI divergence — and an ABC correction is underway. Wave A bottomed at the 16-Jun $3,359 cycle low; wave B failed at $3,406 (22-Jun official). Wave C probed the $2,950–3,080 confluence (.50–.618 span / prior 4th-of-lesser-degree) and printed its low at $3,061.5 (02-Jul); two consecutive higher officials ($3,080 → $3,113 3M through 06-Jul) now give the first firm evidence that C has completed at/near the .50 retracement. Base case (55%): the corrective low is in around $3,060, and a multi-month recovery leg builds toward $3,400–3,600, provided price holds above $3,050. Bear alternate (30%): a lower-degree (b)-wave bounce that rolls over for one more probe of the .618 at $2,894 before the true low. Bull alternate (15%): momentum carries straight back above $3,260 (.382) and the recovery accelerates. Invalidation: a weekly close below $2,850 voids the corrective count and re-opens $2,600. Confidence: moderate, improving with the two-day higher structure.

short_term.label: Short-term — daily (swing degree)
short_term.writeup: Daily (swing): from the $3,855 top, wave A fell to $3,359 (16-Jun), wave B was sold at the $3,607 spike (22-Jun), and wave C unwound to $3,061.5 (02-Jul) — right at the .50 retracement ($3,077) of the whole advance. Two consecutive up-days have followed ($3,087 → $3,113 3M through 06-Jul), the first back-to-back gains since the top, upgrading the read from 'possible' to a confirmed short-term base. Roadmap: while price holds above $3,050 the base case is an extension toward $3,180–3,260, with a clean break of $3,260 (.382) confirming a completed correction (~55%); a stall at $3,120–3,180 and a close back below $3,050 would re-open $2,990–3,020 (~30%); a decisive break of $2,950 exposes $2,894 (.618) (~15%). Invalidation for the base: a daily close below $3,050. Confidence: moderate, up from moderate-low, and firming further above $3,260.

premium_drivers.d: Q3 MJP settled at $395/t (Rio Tinto/South32), UP from Q2's $350–353 and well above the ~$275–320 the market expected — buyers dependent on Middle-East metal paid up despite the LME's ~20% correction; initial producer offers were $460–480.

EGA has ~7% of Al Taweelah pots back online, is AHEAD of its internal restart timeline and expects first alumina from the Al Taweelah refinery early in Q3-2026; Hormuz transit is restored and ~700kt of Gulf metal is framed to return in H2 — the war component of premia unwinds only as fast as pots restart.

50% tariff on full customs value (June-2026 proclamation, eff. 8-Jun-2026) → US Midwest premiums remain structurally elevated (~$0.20–0.21/lb market premium + tariff); the tariff-$ component moves mechanically with the LME (≈$1,557 at the 06-Jul 3M of $3,113).
EU import duty at 6% (reverted from 4% on 1-Jan-2026) plus CBAM carbon costs → European premiums structurally supported despite soft demand.

so_what.line: The correction has turned into a two-day recovery: Monday's 06-Jul official ($3,105 cash / $3,113 3M, +0.8/+0.8%) is the second straight gain and now sits clearly above the $3,077 halfway-retracement, confirming a short-term base against a physical market that still shows Japan's Q3 premium settled UP at $395/t and LME stock drawing below 296kt.
points: Exchange: the latest officials are Monday's — cash $3,105 / 3M $3,113, a second consecutive up-day, with LME stock drawn again to 295,550 t (Westmetall). Today's officials (~13:20 London) test whether the recovery can build toward $3,260 (.382); $3,050 is the line that would re-open the downside.

Base complex firmed with aluminium (Cu 3M $13,370 +0.2%, Zn 3M $3,582 +1.4% and the YTD leader), while Nickel (-0.5%) and Lead (-0.2%) lagged on their surplus stories — a constructive but selective move, not a blanket risk-on.

China stayed firm: SMM A00 spot ¥22,840 (+0.35%, 06-Jul); with the LME up, SHFE now sits ~$151 under the 3M, keeping the import window shut. Last week's ~170 kt warehouse draw — the largest in nearly four years (inventory ~1.165 Mt) — says lower prices keep pulling metal out.

Two-sided still: Q3 MJP settled UP at $395/t and stock is sub-296kt, but EGA runs AHEAD of its Al Taweelah restart (first refinery alumina early Q3), ~700kt of Gulf metal is framed for H2, and freight ticked back up (BDI +2.9% to 2,797). Watch this week: CIF Japan spot convergence to $395; FOMC June minutes Wednesday; China June CPI/PPI Thursday; Alcoa opens Q2 earnings 16-Jul.

exec_summary: The post-conflict correction extended but began to base. LME cash settled at $3,400.00/t on Friday 19 June, down 3.8% on the week from the prior Friday close of $3,536.00 and roughly 12% below the 2 June four-year high of $3,855. The week opened soft (cash $3,419.50 Monday), printed an intraweek low of $3,359.00 on Tuesday as the ceasefire narrative dominated, then stabilised in a $3,400-3,405 band through Wednesday-Friday. Aluminum still holds about +14% year-to-date and ~+33% year-on-year. Crude eased further as the Gulf war-risk premium deflated and a steady dollar (DXY ~100) kept dollar-priced metals on the back foot - but the back-half stabilisation, not the headline drop, is the week's signal.

Under the flat-price weakness, the physical curve quietly re-tightened. The cash-to-3M spread dipped into a ~$30/t contango on Tuesday as de-escalation peaked, then flipped back to a +$2/t backwardation by Friday, while LME stocks drew a further ~4,600 t to ~315,300 t - a multi-year low near 1.3 days of global output. The US-Iran framework was formalised (MOU signed 17 June; official ceremony in Switzerland 19 June) and Hormuz is reopening, but gradually: 25 vessels transited Thursday, the most since April yet far below the ~110/day pre-war norm, and physical metal returns in months not weeks (EGA flagship ~12 months to full capacity, ALBA partially offline). Net read: the war premium has largely unwound, but a persistent deficit and a slow restart are cushioning the downside - a two-sided, range-building market with sharp-rebound risk if the 60-day framework slips.

price_table.note: 19 Jun settlement; week low $3,359 (16 Jun), high $3,419.5 (15 Jun); -12% from 2 Jun peak $3,855
19 Jun official; tracks cash; back-half stabilisation in a $3,398-3,410 band
Dipped to ~-$30 contango (16 Jun) at peak de-escalation, flipped back to +$2 backwardation by 19 Jun - prompt tightness reasserting
19 Jun; -4,625 t on the week; multi-yr low ~1.3 days output; still drawing - deficit persists despite peace narrative
Q2 oversupply persists; energy relief from softer oil adds pressure; Platts/Fastmarkets - verify PRA
Q2 settled $350-353/t (11-yr high, +81% QoQ from Q1 $195); Q3 talks open but Hormuz reopening now caps upside

drivers: US-Iran framework formalised: MOU signed 17 June (Versailles, post-G7), official ceremony in Switzerland Friday 19 June; mediator Pakistan confirmed the US port blockade ceases immediately and Tehran reopens Hormuz - the dominant catalyst that has now largely unwound the conflict premium.

Hormuz reopening is gradual, not instant: 25 commercial vessels transited on Thursday (the most since April) versus a ~110/day pre-war norm; transit requires registration for 'safe passage' (toll-free through the 60-day window), so physical flows normalise over weeks-to-months, not days.

Physical tightness reasserted under the headline: the cash-3M curve flipped from a mid-week ~$30 contango back to a +$2 backwardation by Friday, and LME stocks drew to ~315 kt (~1.3 days output, multi-year low) - the deficit is cushioning the flat-price downside.

Slow supply restart underpins the floor: EGA flagship ~12 months to full capacity, ALBA partially offline; Gulf pre-war supply was ~9% of global / ~25% of non-Chinese output, so the lost tonnage returns slowly even with the Strait open.

Compounding bears cap the upside: rising China output toward the ~45 Mt cap, the Indonesian smelter ramp, RUSAL diverting metal from China to Japan, and Guinea bauxite controls as a modest upstream offset; softer crude and a steady dollar (DXY ~100) round out the macro headwind.

implications.text: the flat-price correction has matured into a base-building range and the curve has re-tightened into backwardation, so prompt material retains a scarcity bid; use the stabilisation to layer disciplined forward hedges on priced tonnes rather than chasing fixed-price sales lower, and preserve value-added premium capture (billet/slab/PFA). Keep optionality for a sharp rebound should the 60-day framework slip.

regional premiums remain elevated (MJP Q2 $350-353/t, an 11-year high; US Midwest firm on Section 232) but a confirmed, sustained Hormuz reopening is the principal downside risk into 2H. Prioritise locking favourable Q3 MJP settlements and renewal terms now, before normalisation and RUSAL's China-to-Japan diversion feed through to premiums.

softer ex-China alumina (~$298-300/t, Q2 oversupply) caps net-seller upside; with vessels resuming Hormuz transit, begin a measured unwind of contingency-routing and war-risk insurance premiums and re-optimise CIF vs FOB exposure - but keep committed-cargo vessel cover until shipowners validate sustained safe passage (transit is rebounding but still well below pre-war volumes).

hold OTIF for strategic accounts through the transition and track the credibility/mechanics of the 60-day window, Hormuz transit counts (the real normalisation tell), EGA/ALBA restart cadence, LME stock direction and the cash-3M curve (the real tightness tell), China export/cap enforcement and the Indonesian ramp, plus US CPI/PPI and the Fed for the dollar.

outlook: Hormuz transit normalisation - whether daily vessel counts keep climbing toward the ~110/day pre-war norm and shipowners validate sustained safe passage; a stall would re-introduce a supply premium.

The cash-3M curve and LME stocks - continued draws and a holding/steepening backwardation would confirm the deficit persists despite the peace narrative; a flip back to contango would signal the unwind resuming.
Physical restart cadence - EGA/ALBA repair milestones gate how fast Gulf tonnage actually returns; the gap between visible and invisible inventories remains the key tightness risk.

Macro and competing supply - the 60-day framework's durability (slippage is the primary upside-price risk), China output against the ~45 Mt cap, the Indonesian ramp, and US CPI/PPI and the Fed for USD direction.
weekly.sources: LME official cash/3-month settlements and warehouse stock via Westmetall (week of 15-19 June: cash $3,400.0, 3M $3,398.0, stock 315,300 t on 19 Jun); benchmark CFD price (~$3,400.85 on 19 Jun, ~+33% YoY), USD/DXY, energy and macro via Trading Economics; US-Iran MOU / Strait of Hormuz reopening, 17 June signing and 19 June Switzerland ceremony, and Thursday's 25-vessel transit via Al Jazeera, NBC News, PBS, Seatrade Maritime, CNN and the UK House of Commons Library (14-19 June); EGA/ALBA restart timelines and Gulf supply share via Trading Economics and Fastmarkets; MJP Q2 premium (~$350-353/t, +81% QoQ) and RUSAL China-to-Japan diversion via S&P Global Platts, Discovery Alert and AlCircle; alumina FOB Australia via Fastmarkets and S&P Global Platts.
weekly.caveat: Note: 19 June SHFE, alumina and physical-premium figures are estimates or most-recent public anchors; daily PRA assessments (Platts/Fastmarkets/Argus) and SHFE/SMM quotes sit behind licensed feeds and should be reconciled to the desk's licensed data before pricing. This brief is market intelligence, not trading advice.
